newTests.name: Interactive overlay element picker. > the connect modal dismisses on Escape
Read-only inspection commands. > bounding_box returns numeric geometry
Overflow + clipping detection, the stuff CDP-based tooling gives us. > is_clipped detects an element overflowing its scroll parent

A shared token gates browser<->bridge traffic: the right token round-trips,
   a missing/wrong one is refused with 403 before it reaches the tab. > authorizes a tab carrying the token and refuses requests without it
Storage, evaluate, and page meta. > storage set/get/clear round-trips
Engine installs and answers to meta commands. > returns ok:false for an unknown action

`route-command!` is the CLI-side client: it POSTs one command to the bridge's
   /command endpoint and gets the browser's result back in daemon shape. This is
   exactly the path a regular `spel <verb>` follows once a target is saved. > routes a command to a live tab and adapts the result
A real browser subscribes over SSE and answers commands pushed by the server. > serves the engine over HTTP and the tab installs the api
fetch / XMLHttpRequest are wrapped and surfaced via network_* commands. > captures a fetch() with method, status and response body
Click / fill / type / press / check / select against the live DOM. > select picks an option by value

The saved target profile round-trips and drives which transport a command
   takes (daemon vs. bridge). > save/load/clear a bridge target on an explicit path
Overflow + clipping detection, the stuff CDP-based tooling gives us. > is_overflowing detects a scroll container
fetch / XMLHttpRequest are wrapped and surfaced via network_* commands. > network_clear empties the window
Accessibility snapshot assigns refs and describes the tree. > refs resolve back to elements for actions
Accessibility snapshot assigns refs and describes the tree. > produces a tree with roles, names and refs

emulate overrides what page JS reads (no CDP): geolocation / timezone /
   locale / device / prefers-color-scheme. > overrides geolocation, locale and device metrics

spel-sw.js registers, controls the page, and forwards passive subresources
   (which the fetch/XHR wrappers never see) into the network capture. > captures a passive <script> subresource via the service worker
alert/confirm/prompt are intercepted, follow a policy and are recorded. > dismisses confirm/prompt under the dismiss policy
cookies + page.route()-style fetch mocking against a real HTTP origin. > sets, reads and clears cookies

screenshot serializes the DOM into an SVG (and rasterizes to PNG when the
   canvas is not tainted) — pure JS, no CDP. > returns an SVG data URL sized to the element
Engine installs and answers to meta commands. > responds to ping/ready
Read-only inspection commands. > get_text / get_attribute / count
Playwright-style `>>` locator chaining and index/text/visibility filters. > resolves nth / first / last / has-text / visible over a matched set
wait_for_function / _load_state / _timeout resolve on real conditions. > wait_for_timeout just delays
network_har serializes the in-page capture as HAR 1.2. > emits a HAR log with a real captured entry
wait_for polls for element state and resolves/times out. > waits for an element that appears later
cookies + page.route()-style fetch mocking against a real HTTP origin. > wait_for_response resolves on a matching later response
Click / fill / type / press / check / select against the live DOM. > type appends character by character
wait_for polls for element state and resolves/times out. > resolves immediately for an already-visible element
is_* / property / aria queries against the live DOM. > is_editable / is_disabled / is_hidden / is_focused

spel-sw.js registers, controls the page, and forwards passive subresources
   (which the fetch/XHR wrappers never see) into the network capture. > sw_status reports control and sw_unregister tears it down
Engine installs and answers to meta commands. > installs window.__spel and reports a version
cookies + page.route()-style fetch mocking against a real HTTP origin. > route abort rejects the fetch
Click / fill / type / press / check / select against the live DOM. > scroll moves a scrollable container
The bridge exposes the embedded service worker source (spel-sw.js). > sw-source returns the installable spel-sw.js
same-origin iframe drill-down via frame= selectors. > lists frames and acts inside a same-origin iframe
The ejected loader/bookmarklet target the right origin + connect URL. > eject-origin defaults to the serving host/port, splits an explicit url

A real browser subscribes over SSE and answers commands pushed by the server. > browser subscribes and round-trips invoke commands

emulate overrides what page JS reads (no CDP): geolocation / timezone /
   locale / device / prefers-color-scheme. > overrides the reported timezone for Intl
wait_for polls for element state and resolves/times out. > times out and reports ok:false when the element never appears
Click / fill / type / press / check / select against the live DOM. > check and uncheck toggle a checkbox
Click / fill / type / press / check / select against the live DOM. > press Backspace edits the focused input

The engine survives a full-page navigation: its connect route (url + token)
   is persisted per-tab in sessionStorage, so a real reload re-subscribes
   automatically instead of losing the tab. > persists the route, clears it on disconnect, and re-subscribes on reload
Interactive overlay element picker. > clicking during a pick records the selected element
wait_for_function / _load_state / _timeout resolve on real conditions. > wait_for_function times out to ok:false
console.* and page errors are captured after console_capture. > captures console messages and filters by level
alert/confirm/prompt are intercepted, follow a policy and are recorded. > accepts confirm and records the dialog by default
The bridge exposes the embedded engine source. > engine-source returns the installable spel.js
set_input_files, dispatch_event and tap drive real DOM state. > tap clicks through pointer events
Click / fill / type / press / check / select against the live DOM. > click resolves text= selectors
The ejected loader/bookmarklet target the right origin + connect URL. > loader-script injects spel.js and connects; bookmarklet just prefixes it

`route-command!` is the CLI-side client: it POSTs one command to the bridge's
   /command endpoint and gets the browser's result back in daemon shape. This is
   exactly the path a regular `spel <verb>` follows once a target is saved. > reports a clear error when no bridge is listening

cookies + page.route()-style fetch mocking against a real HTTP origin. > route fulfills a fetch without hitting the network
